title: run_verify.mjs supports a scoped include filter (--only / BEE_VERIFY_ONLY)
action: Per verify-scoping D1. Add an include filter to scripts/run_verify.mjs: a repeatable/comma-separated `--only <token>` CLI flag plus a `BEE_VERIFY_ONLY` env var (CLI wins when both are set). A token matches a runnable by case-insensitive substring against its repo-relative path AND its display name, covering both glob-discovered suites and EXTRA_SUITES entries. The include filter applies BEFORE BEE_VERIFY_EXCLUDE (exclude still drops from the included set). Zero matches = typed refusal, exit 1, mirroring the existing BEE_VERIFY_ROOT_FILTER refusal at scripts/run_verify.mjs:445-450,557-563. Every scoped run prints a loud banner before results and again in the summary: 'SCOPED RUN (--only): N of M runnables selected — full verify still required before close'. With no --only/env, behavior is byte-identical to today. Serial-sensitive and live-bundle grouping semantics still apply to whatever subset remains. Write scripts/test_run_verify_only.mjs (model it on scripts/test_run_verify_skip_marker.mjs): assert (a) --only selects the expected subset and passes, (b) zero-match refusal exits 1 with the typed message, (c) the SCOPED RUN banner appears on scoped runs and never on full runs, (d) BEE_VERIFY_ONLY env works and CLI --only overrides it. Keep each test invocation cheap: filter to a single fast suite (e.g. test_release_tuple) so the test itself stays seconds, not minutes.
must_haves: no flag/env => byte-identical current behavior (no banner)
--only and BEE_VERIFY_ONLY both work; CLI wins
matches path AND display name, case-insensitive substring, EXTRA_SUITES included
SCOPED RUN banner on every scoped run
new test scripts/test_run_verify_only.mjs covers selection, refusal, banner, env-vs-CLI precedence

trace.outcome: run_verify.mjs gained --only/BEE_VERIFY_ONLY scoped include filter with zero-match refusal and SCOPED RUN banner; 8-case test green
